title: roasted shandong lemongrass-takrai roast
description: a rich and savory chinese-inspired roasted dish with a lemongrass and takrai twist, perfect for a midnight craving. this vegan-friendly recipe brings a complexity of smoke, spice, and umami flavors together in perfect harmony.

ingredients.name: lemongrass
ingredients.notes: use fresh lemongrass for the best flavor

ingredients.name: takrai
ingredients.notes: also known as galangal, use fresh for the most intense flavor

ingredients.name: sweet potatoes
ingredients.notes: high in fiber and nutrients, adds natural sweetness to the dish

ingredients.name: tofu
ingredients.notes: marry tofu with the smoky flavors of the dish for a satisfying protein source

ingredients.name: smoked paprika
ingredients.notes: adds a deep, smoky flavor to the dish

ingredients.name: soy sauce
ingredients.notes: use reduced-sodium soy sauce for a healthier option

ingredients.name: rice vinegar
ingredients.notes: adds a tangy, slightly sweet flavor to the dish

ingredients.name: oyster-free mushroom sauce
ingredients.notes: adds depth and umami flavor to the dish

ingredients.name: black pepper
ingredients.notes: adds a bit of heat and warmth to the dish

ingredients.name: sesame oil
ingredients.notes: adds a nutty, aromatic flavor to the dish

instructions: peel and chop the sweet potatoes into large cubes, and place them on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
in a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, rice vinegar, and sesame oil to create a marinade for the tofu.
add the tofu to the marinade and toss to coat.
chop the lemongrass into small pieces and add it to the tofu mixture, along with smoked paprika and black pepper.
massage the marinade into the sweet potatoes and tofu mixture, making sure everything is well coated.
place the lemongrass-takrai mixture on top of the sweet potatoes, followed by the block of tofu.
roast the dish in the preheated oven for 35 minutes, or until the sweet potatoes are tender and the tofu is crispy on the outside.
remove the dish from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes before serving.
serve the roasted shandong lemongrass-takrai roast hot, garnished with chopped green onions and sesame seeds (optional).
